text:
St Mary's Hospital 

Institute of Neurology 

Clinic Date: 14 October 2025 

Dr Singh  
St Mary's Hospital  
Institute of Neurology, St Mary's Hospital, London W2 1NY  

Dear Dr Singh,  

Daniel Morgan, DOB: 11-02-2002, Hospital No: H938427 NHS No. 9834156207  
Flat 3, 12 Brookside Road, London, SW7 4DL 

Medication: 

No regular antiseizure medication at present. Uses melatonin 2 mg PRN discussed but not commenced.

Background and interval history: 

He is the lead of a university esports club, with prolonged screen exposure and irregular sleep around tournaments and late-evening practice sessions. He reports implementing structured breaks, blue-light filtering, and a fixed cut-off for gaming on weeknights. He has also begun using a sleep-tracking app to stabilise his bedtime. 

Current status: 

Since our last review there have been no witnessed events or self-reported episodes concerning for seizures. He has had occasional brief light-headedness after extended screen time, resolving with hydration and rest, without any associated loss of awareness, abnormal movements, or post-event confusion. There have been no emergency attendances, and no nocturnal events reported by his flatmates. 

Examination today was unremarkable. We reviewed general lifestyle measures including regular sleep, hydration, and taking short breaks during extended gaming sessions. He prefers to remain off medication at this stage; this is reasonable given his stable course and absence of events. He will continue to monitor with a simple event log should any concerning symptoms arise. 

Plan: 
- Continue non-pharmacological strategies (sleep regularity, screen breaks, hydration). 
- No driving implications discussed today as he remains asymptomatic; he understands to inform DVLA and us immediately should any events occur. 
- No routine bloods required. EEG/MRI not repeated at this stage given stable course; we will reconsider if there is any change. 
- Follow-up in 9 months, sooner if there are any new symptoms. 

Yours sincerely

entities.text: Since our last review there have been no witnessed events or self-reported episodes concerning for seizures.

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states there have been no witnessed events or self-reported episodes concerning for seizures since the last review. No exact duration since the last review is provided, but the current status indicates ongoing absence of seizures. To classify as 'seizure free' under the rules, there must be at least 6 months of seizure freedom; if the exact duration is not stated but suggests ongoing absence, we can use 'seizure free for multiple months'. There is no mention of any seizures occurring in the interval, only non-seizure symptoms (light-headedness) without features of seizures.
seizure_frequency_number: Since our last review there have been no witnessed events or self-reported episodes concerning for seizures.
